name: Lobster and Confetti Vegetable Salad
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 tablespoons chopped fresh tarragon, or 1 1/2 teaspoons dried tarragon
1 large white-skinned potato, cut into 1/2-inch pieces
1 large ear fresh corn, kernels cut from cob
recipeInstructions:
1. Place mustard in a small bowl. Whisk together 3 tablespoons rice vinegar and 2 tablespoons orange juice.
2. Gradually whisk in olive oil. Add chopped tarragon (or dried), shallot, and orange peel. Season dressing to taste with salt and pepper.
3. Char bell peppers over gas flame or under broiler until blackened on all sides. Place in a paper bag and let stand 10 minutes. Peel and seed the peppers; cut into 1/2-inch pieces. Add to a large bowl.
4. Bring a large pot of salted water to boil. Add lobster tails and cook for 10 minutes. Transfer to a bowl of ice water and cool.
5. Add green beans to the boiling water and cook until crisp-tender, about 5 minutes. Using a slotted spoon, transfer beans to another bowl of ice water and cool.
6. Add potato to the pot of boiling water and cook for 2 minutes. Add corn kernels; cook until potato and corn are just tender, about 2 more minutes. Drain well and add to the bowl with peppers.
7. Drain green beans and pat dry with paper towels. Add beans to the potato mixture.
8. Remove lobster meat from shells and cut crosswise into rounds. Mix half of the dressing into the salad.
9. Toss greens with remaining 1 tablespoon rice vinegar. Place on a large platter. Mount the salad onto the greens. Arrange lobster over the greens and serve.
Notes: The lobster and vegetables are tossed with an orange, shallot, and tarragon dressing, then served over mixed greens. Offer lavash crackers and white wine spritzers.
